topic: Definition of Relation and Function, Domain, Range, and Notations

questions.question: Which of the following relations is a function?

options.A: A relation with two inputs having the same output
options.B: A relation with one input having two different outputs
options.C: A relation where each input corresponds to exactly one output
options.D: A relation with no inputs or outputs
options.E: A relation with only one input

questions.question: What is the domain of the function $f(x) = \frac{1}{x}$?
The range of the function $g(x) = x^2$ is
In the context of planetary motion, if $d$ represents the distance of a planet from the sun, and $t$ represents time, which of the following could be a function?
The notation $f(2)$ represents the value of the function $f$ at
Which of the following statements about the function $h(x) = x^3$ is true?

options.A: The domain is all real numbers, and the range is all positive real numbers
options.B: The domain is all positive real numbers, and the range is all real numbers
options.C: The domain and range are both all real numbers
options.D: The domain is all negative real numbers, and the range is all positive real numbers
options.E: The domain and range are both limited to $x = 0$

questions.question: For the relation given by the set of ordered pairs $\{(1, 2), (2, 3), (3, 2), (4, 5)\}$, which of the following is true?

options.A: It is a function because each input corresponds to exactly one output
options.B: It is a function because it has four distinct outputs
options.C: It is not a function because one input corresponds to two different outputs
options.D: It is not a function because two inputs correspond to the same output
options.E: It is neither a function nor a relation

questions.question: Given the graph of a function, how can you determine if it is a function using the vertical line test?

options.A: By drawing a horizontal line that intersects the graph at more than one point
options.B: By drawing a vertical line that intersects the graph at exactly one point
options.C: By drawing a vertical line that intersects the graph at more than one point
options.D: By drawing a diagonal line that intersects the graph

questions.question: A space mission involves launching a satellite into orbit around the Earth. The relationship between the distance $d$ (in miles) of the satellite from the center of the Earth and the time $t$ (in hours) after launch can be modeled by the function $d(t) = 2000 + 500t$. Determine the domain and range of this function, assuming the satellite travels in a straight line and does not change direction. Provide your answer in the space below, clearly labeling the domain and range.

A lab experiment measures the velocity $v$ (in meters per second) of a particle as a function of time $t$ (in seconds) using the equation $v(t) = 2t^2 - 5t + 1$. Evaluate $v(3)$ and explain what this value represents in the context of the experiment. Provide your calculations and explanation in the space below.

teacher_rules: Emphasize the importance of understanding function notation.
Use visual aids to illustrate key concepts like the vertical line test.
Encourage students to use real-world examples to reinforce their understanding of functions.
